title: Nightly memory consolidation: one pass unifying caretaker + retention + replay + promotion
current_state: The consolidation pieces exist but run ad-hoc and separately: caretaker (contradictions, derived facts), retention (decay/prune), idle_replay (default-off rehearsal), promote_l2_to_l3 (mid-session + exit). No single owner; ordering between them is undefined; nothing runs them when Pi is idle overnight.
target_state: One idempotent scripts/consolidate.py running nightly (T-202 scheduler slot): 1) caretaker full (contradictions resolved, derived facts recomputed) → 2) L2 semantic dedup sweep → 3) promotion pass (L2→L3 by importance) → 4) retention decay/prune (L1 30d, stale L3) → 5) pattern detection (the idle_replay detect_cross_session_patterns query) writing pattern_observation facts. Each step logged with counts to logs/consolidation.jsonl; Telegram one-liner ('consolidated: 3 contradictions, 12 promoted, 41 pruned').
migration_plan: 1. scripts/consolidate.py orchestrating existing functions in the fixed order above — no new memory logic, just ownership + ordering.
2. Lock file so consolidation never overlaps a live session's mid-session distill (skip + log if agent active within 10 min).
3. Dry-run flag printing what WOULD change (trust-building period).
4. Tests: order enforced, lock respected, idempotent double-run (second run is all no-ops), counts logged.
risk_notes: Memory is RISK_FLAGGED territory — ship with --dry-run as default for the first week; Ash flips to live after reviewing a few dry reports. The write/read-divergence class lives here; every step must reuse the EXISTING tested functions, never reimplement.
